message:
fix(mail): make LIFECYCLE and merge protocol messages ephemeral wisps (gt-vpy9)

LIFECYCLE:Shutdown messages were created as permanent beads in HQ, accumulating
as open issues when polecats get recycled. These are transient protocol signals
that should be ephemeral wisps.

Add lifecycle:, merged, merge_ready, and merge_failed to the shouldBeWisp()
auto-detection prefixes so all protocol messages are stored as ephemeral wisps
rather than permanent beads.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -221,6 +221,31 @@ func TestShouldBeWisp(t *testing.T) {
 			msg:  &Message{Subject: "Please review this PR"},
 			want: false,
 		},
+		{
+			name: "LIFECYCLE:Shutdown subject",
+			msg:  &Message{Subject: "LIFECYCLE:Shutdown capable"},
+			want: true,
+		},
+		{
+			name: "LIFECYCLE: polecat requesting shutdown",
+			msg:  &Message{Subject: "LIFECYCLE: polecat-nux requesting shutdown"},
+			want: true,
+		},
+		{
+			name: "MERGED subject",
+			msg:  &Message{Subject: "MERGED nux"},
+			want: true,
+		},
+		{
+			name: "MERGE_READY subject",
+			msg:  &Message{Subject: "MERGE_READY nux"},
+			want: true,
+		},
+		{
+			name: "MERGE_FAILED subject",
+			msg:  &Message{Subject: "MERGE_FAILED nux"},
+			want: true,
+		},
 		{
 			name: "handoff message (not auto-wisp)",
 			msg:  &Message{Subject: "HANDOFF: context notes"},
